pattern: Combine three needs - reach length 6..20, cover the three character classes, and break every run of three identical characters - by spending replacements on the runs first, because one replacement can simultaneously fix a run and add a missing class.
hints: Separate the problem into three independent shortfalls: characters needed to reach length six, distinct character types still missing, and replacements needed to shatter every run of three or more identical characters.
When the password is short, insertions both add length and can split runs, so the answer is the larger of the length deficit and the missing-type count.

When the password is long, deletions must trim it to twenty; spend deletions cleverly on runs whose length mod 3 is 0 then 1 to reduce the replacements those runs would otherwise cost, then combine with the missing-type count.

edge_inputs.description: single character needs length and all three classes
all classes present but too short
long enough but one run of three to break
too long with overlapping long runs and missing classes
over twenty needing deletions plus a missing class
short with a triple run, insertion both lengthens and splits
exactly six with a triple run but all classes present

stress.description: random passwords across the legal alphabet and lengths, exercising all three regimes
stress.note: the repeat bias produces frequent runs of three so the replacement logic is exercised
